steps.title: Find the central claim
steps.content: The passage focuses on Lawson-Grosvenor's book Vibration Cooking: it was her most influential project, unusual for its time, and influential in food writing.

steps.title: Reject distortions
steps.content: Choice B shifts focus to her career generally. Choice C contradicts the statement that the book was long admired. Choice D calls her a chef and claims she inspired home cooking, neither of which is the passage's focus.

steps.title: Choose the main idea
steps.content: Choice A captures the book's unusual form and lasting importance to writing about cuisine, which is the main point of the text. The answer is A.
